channel.title: Mozilla-Hacks on Daily Tech Articles Feed
channel.description: Recent content in Mozilla-Hacks on Daily Tech Articles Feed

item.title: Launching Interop 2026
item.description: 🚀 The Interop Project aims to enhance web compatibility for users and developers alike. Key players like Apple, Google, Microsoft, and Mozilla are collaborating to evaluate proposals based on stable standards and comprehensive test coverage. Their focus is on balancing developer needs with available resources. 📊 Looking back at Interop 2025, the overall score improved from 25 to 95, highlighting significant advancements in cross-browser features such as Same-Document View Transitions and CSS…

item.title: CRLite: Fast, private, and comprehensive certificate revocation checking in Firefox
item.description: 🚀 Firefox has launched CRLite, the first fast and private certificate revocation checking system that protects user browsing activity. This new mechanism downloads a compact list of revoked certificates, making checking efficient and reliable. Starting with Firefox 137, CRLite enhances security while disabling the less private OCSP method in future updates. #Firefox #CRLite #Cybersecurity #Privacy #WebDevelopment
